title: Skill-chain definition
description: Validates a chain definition file. Transferable chains live at <repo>/chains/<name>.yaml; proprietary chains live at <project>/.claude/chains/<name>.yaml.

name.description: kebab-case identifier; must match the file basename (without .yaml).

description.description: What this chain accomplishes end-to-end. Specific enough that a user picking from a list can choose correctly.

skills.description: Ordered list of skill names. The chain runner invokes each in sequence; non-zero exit aborts the rest. The project's auto-supervisor (if any) is appended automatically unless --no-supervisor is passed.

transferable.description: PROPRIETARY chains only: the name of the transferable chain this is a project-specific instantiation of. Informational (no inheritance / override behavior in MVP); used by the supervisor to correlate cycles across projects that follow the same conceptual flow.

transferable-version.description: PROPRIETARY chains only: npm-style range pinning the transferable counterpart.

auto-supervisor.description: If false, the chain runner does NOT auto-append the project's supervisor. Equivalent to passing --no-supervisor on the CLI. Default true.

user-invocable.description: If false, this chain is intended to be triggered only by another chain or a higher-level orchestrator, not directly by the user.

loop.description: Default iteration count for this chain. 1 = run once (default, current behavior). N > 1 = run the full skill sequence N times. 0 = loop until a non-supervisor skill fails or the user interrupts. CLI flag --loop overrides this field.

loop-delay.description: Seconds to sleep between iterations when loop != 1. Ignored for single-iteration runs. CLI flag --loop-delay overrides this field. Mutually exclusive with loop-delay-random.

loop-delay-random.description: Per-iteration randomized delay [min_seconds, max_seconds]. When set, the runner samples a uniform-random duration in [min, max] inclusive after each iteration boundary (instead of the fixed loop-delay value). Mutually exclusive with loop-delay; setting both is a YAML validation error. CLI flag --loop-delay-random overrides this field. Rationale: a fixed cadence on a multi-iter run is detectable as automation; jittering between e.g. [60, 3600] makes the trace indistinguishable from human-driven workflow.

auto-promote.description: Controls where the supervisor writes proposed SKILL.md rewrites. 'off': writes to <skill-dir>/SKILL.patch.md (one per skill, overwritten each run); user promotes manually via /sst-promote-skill-proposal. 'proprietary' (default): supervisor overwrites proprietary SKILL.md files in <cwd>/.claude/skills/ directly so the next iteration sees the improved prose; transferables still go to SKILL.patch.md for human review. 'all': also overwrites transferable SKILL.md files under ~/.claude/skills/, but only after sst-sanitize-transferable reports must-fix=0; otherwise that skill falls back to SKILL.patch.md with a warning. Rollback of an unwanted auto-applied change is `git checkout <path>/SKILL.md`.

on-rate-limit.description: How to handle Anthropic rate-limit signals (rate_limit_event with status=exceeded/blocked/reset_required, or stderr/exit-code fallback). 'fail' (legacy): a rate-limit kills the chain like any other non-zero exit. 'pause' (default): sleep until the reset_time + jitter, then re-invoke the killed skill from scratch (each invocation is its own subprocess; no in-process state to preserve). 'pause-with-cap': same as 'pause' but if a single pause would exceed `max-rate-limit-pause-seconds`, fall back to fail. CLI flag --on-rate-limit overrides this field.

max-rate-limit-pause-seconds.description: Upper bound (seconds) on a single rate-limit pause when on-rate-limit is 'pause-with-cap'. Default 28800 = 8h covers the standard 5h rolling window plus headroom. Ignored for 'fail' / 'pause'. CLI flag --max-rate-limit-pause-seconds overrides this field.

max-pauses-per-session.description: Hard cap on rate-limit pauses per skill within one chain invocation. Repeated pauses on the same skill suggest a quota-burning loop, not a genuine quota crossing — abort the chain after this many. Default 3. CLI flag --max-pauses-per-session overrides this field.
